questions.question_text: If $\sqrt{x}$ of $2601=(\sqrt{1089}+\sqrt{324}) \times 35$. Find the value of $x$.
A store sells pens and notebooks. If 3 pens and 2 notebooks cost ₹120, and 2 pens and 4 notebooks cost ₹140, what is the price of one notebook?

A shopkeeper wants to earn a profit of $11\frac{1}{9}\%$ even after giving two successive discounts of $16\frac{2}{3}\%$ and $20\%$ on the marked price of an article. If the cost price of the article for the shopkeeper is ₹1800, what should its marked price be?
If the third proportional to two numbers is 32 and the second number is 16, find the first number.

A shopkeeper offers the following schemes on toys of the same marked price.(A) Successive discounts of 25% and 3% on any number of toys bought.(B) Successive discounts of 18%, 13% and 5% on any number of toys bought.(C) 18% discount on the first 2 toys and 41% discount on each toy thereon.(D) 4 toys free for buying 4 toys in bulk.A customer wants to buy 7 toys. Which of the above schemes is the least beneficial to him?
The salary of an employee is increased by 5% in the first year and again by 5% in the second year. What is the overall percentage increase in the salary after two years?
A 104-metre-long train crosses another 84-metre-long train running in the same direction in 16.2 seconds. If the speed of the first train is 61 kmph, what is the speed of the second train in kmph?
Ravi spends 70% of his income. If his income increases by 25% and his expenditure increases by 10%, by what percentage do his savings increase?
The fourth proportional to 1.2, 4.8, and 7.15 is:
Given the data set: 12, 36, 22, 27, 31, 17. What is the median value?
The total surface area of a cube having side 86 cm is:
A quadrilateral has one diagonal of length 36 cm, and the perpendiculars from the other two vertices to this diagonal are 20 cm and 10 cm. What is the area of this quadrilateral?

During a sale, 40% of the goods are sold at a profit of 10%. Of the remaining goods, 25% are sold at a profit of 20%, and the rest are sold at a loss of 20%. If there is an overall loss of x%, find the value of x.
Two numbers are in the ratio 4 : 5. If their LCM is 240, what is the sum of the two numbers?
The sum of the present ages of a mother and her daughter is 50 years. Five years ago, the mother's age was 7 times the daughter's age. What is the present age of the mother?

A rectangular garden has a length of 25 meters and a width of 18 meters. If a path of uniform width 0.5 meters is constructed around the outside of the garden, what is the perimeter of the outer edge of the path?
A train, 380 meters long, crosses a stationary train of length 340 m in 16 seconds. Find the speed of the train.
Simplify: $$\frac{(1.5 \times 0.2)}{0.3}$$
The cost price of 25 articles is the same as the selling price of x articles. If the profit is 25%, find the value of x is:
A tank is normally filled by pipe A in 10 hours. Due to a leak, the tank takes 12 hours to fill. Find the time taken by the leak alone to empty a full tank.
The mean proportional between two positive numbers is 30. If the difference between the two numbers is 45, what is the smaller of the two numbers?
If a sum of ₹70,000 amounts to ₹74,263 in X years under compound interest, compounded annually at 3% per annum, find X.
If ₹25,200 is invested at a simple interest rate of 6% per annum for 4 years, find the interest earned.
A and B can do a piece of work in 12 days and 36 days, respectively. Both work for 4 days and then A leaves. How long (in days) will B take to complete the remaining work?

Seven boxes A, B, C, D, E, F and G are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only two boxes are kept below A. Only one box is kept above G. Only one box is kept between E and B. C is kept immediately above G. D is kept at some place below A. E is kept at some place below B. B is kept immediately above E. D is kept at some place below C. Which box is kept at the top?

Which is the one that does NOT belong to that group?(Note: Add one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)

T sits to the immediate right of B. B sits at the immediate left of M and M sits second to the left of Z. Who sits at the extreme left end of the row?

E.g. 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding / subtracting / multiplying to 13 etc. (Operations like adding 1 and 3 into 13 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed).)
18, 325
19, 362

Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. You have to take the given statements to be true even if it appears to be at variance from commonly known facts. You have to decide which conclusions logically follow/s from the given statements.
Statements: Some cups are jugs. Some jugs are vases.
Conclusion (I): All vases are jugs.
Conclusion (II): All vases are cups.
If IP 18 is related to KL 4 in a certain way. In the same way, KL 16 is related to MH 2. To which of the following is AT 9 related, following the same logic?

Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s).
Statements:
Some papers are rains.
All rains are trees.
Conclusions:
(I) Some papers are trees.
(II) All trees are rains.

She takes a final right turn, drives 3 km and stops at Point B. How far (shortest distance) and in which direction Nova should drive to reach Point A again? (All turns are 90-degree turns only unless specified.)

Which of the following letters will be fourth from the left in the new group of letters thus formed?

Seven people, B, C, D, E, F, G and H, are sitting in a row, facing north. Only C sits to the left of B. Only four people sit between D and T. Only C sits between J and R, and I is not an immediate neighbor of T. Who sits at the extreme right of the line?

Seven boxes B, C, D, E, Q, R and S are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only five boxes are kept above D. Only one box is kept above Q. E is kept at one of the positions above C. How many boxes are kept between Q and S?

In a certain code language, 'A + B means 'A is the son of B'.'A - B means 'A is the wife of B'.'A $\times$ B means 'A is the wife of B'.'A $\div$ B means 'A is the father of B'.
Based on the above, how is S related to H if 'S + D - F + H $\times$ K'?

NFG 49, PIK 36, RLO 25, TOS 16, ?

In a certain code language,
J $\div$ Q means 'J is the sister of Q'.
J + Q means 'J is the mother of Q'.
J - Q means 'J is the husband of Q' and
J $\times$ Q means 'J is the brother of Q'.
Based on the above, how is S related to W if 'S + T - U + V $\times$ W Y'?

EAW 91 XTP 98 QMI 105 JFB 112 ?
